headline: Cuypers holds one-goal edge at the top
dek: The Chicago striker leads at 13, but Musa and Messi are a single finish behind with plenty of season remaining.
body: Hugo Cuypers sits at 13 goals for Chicago Fire FC, one clear of Petar Musa (FC Dallas) and Leo Messi (Inter Miami CF), who are level on 12. Messi complicates the picture further by ranking second in assists with seven, making him the race's most versatile threat. Cuypers holds the advantage for now, but the margin is thin enough that a quiet week from Chicago could hand the lead to either challenger.

headline: Son leads assists; Messi and Dreyer give chase
dek: LAFC's Son Heung-Min sits on eight assists, one ahead of a pair of players separated by nothing at all.
body: Son Heung-Min has distributed eight assists for LAFC, the most in the league at this stage. Behind him, Leo Messi of Inter Miami CF and Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC are deadlocked on seven. San Diego's expansion-season production through Dreyer is a notable data point. Son needs only to maintain his current rate to hold the lead, but the gap is narrow enough that one decisive performance changes the order.

headline: Ingvartsen and White sit at opposite extremes
dek: San Diego's Ingvartsen is outscoring his xG by 4.3 goals; Vancouver's White is leaving 2.4 on the table.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen leads all overperformers with 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a positive difference of plus-4.3. Houston's Guilherme is close behind at plus-4.2 on just 3.8 expected goals. At the other end, Brian White of Vancouver has scored 10 but carries an xG of 12.4, a gap of minus-2.4. Minnesota's Kelvin Yeboah trails his model by minus-1.8. Both underperformers remain productive in volume terms, which softens the concern.

headline: Nashville defense sets the standard; San Jose leads West
dek: Nashville's 11 goals allowed is the best defensive record in MLS, while Inter Miami's attack has produced 39 goals, most in the league.
body: Nashville SC tops the East on 33 points with a 10-3-1 record and have conceded only 11 goals across 14 matches, the stingiest mark in the league. San Jose Earthquakes lead the West on 32 points at 10-2-3 and have scored 34 times. Inter Miami CF own the most potent attack in MLS with 39 goals for, though their defensive numbers do not appear in the top marks. The two conference leaders are separated by a single point.

headline: Cuypers leads, but Musa and Messi breathe down his neck
dek: One goal separates Chicago's Belgian striker from a two-man pack at 12 apiece.
body: Hugo Cuypers sits atop the MLS scoring chart with 13 goals for Chicago Fire FC. Petar Musa of FC Dallas and Inter Miami's Leo Messi share second place at 12. Messi also carries a parallel threat in the assists column, where he trails only LAFC's Son Heung-Min, 8 to 7. Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC matches Messi's seven helpers. The golden boot picture will tighten fast if Cuypers hits a dry spell.

headline: Ingvartsen and Guilherme beating the numbers by wide margins
dek: Two attackers are scoring far above what the model expects, while White and Yeboah owe the ledger goals.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen of San Diego FC leads the league's xG overperformers with 11 goals against an expected 6.8, a gap of plus-4.3. Houston's Guilherme sits just behind at plus-4.2, having converted 8 goals from chances worth only 3.8. On the other side, Vancouver's Brian White has 10 goals but an xG of 12.4, leaving him minus-2.4. Minnesota's Kelvin Yeboah trails his xG by 1.8. Regression pressure on both underperformers is real.

headline: Cubas at 52 tackles, a clear margin over the field
dek: Vancouver's holding midfielder is setting a punishing defensive pace midseason.
body: Andrés Cubas of the Vancouver Whitecaps leads all MLS players with 52 tackles recorded. Inter Miami's Yannick Bright is second at 47, and D.C. United's João Peglow sits third at 45. The five-tackle gap between Cubas and Bright is notable at this stage of the campaign. All three players operate in central or defensive-midfield roles, reflecting where teams are investing their pressing and recovery work this season.

headline: Nashville and San Jose hold conference leads on 33 and 32 pts
dek: Nashville's defense is the stingiest in MLS; Miami's attack has no equal in goals scored.
body: Nashville SC lead the Eastern Conference with 33 points from a 10-3-1 record, conceding just 11 goals, the best defensive return in the league. San Jose Earthquakes top the West on 32 points at 10-2-3, though their 15 goals allowed show more vulnerability at the back. Inter Miami CF hold the league's most productive attack with 39 goals scored, nine more than San Jose's conference-leading total of 34.
